Off Calendar Mar-22-2010 Continued To May-03-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Diana Carloni appeared by telephone.

Ms. Carloni stated that the sale of the property fell through, that she would proceed with closing the Estate and distribute the property to the Siblings. The matter was continued to May 3, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Clauzel.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1076 Tiajuana Street, San Bernardino, Ca)
Off Calendar Nov-02-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Diana Carloni for Donna Davis.

There being no overbids, the sale was confirmed at $55,000.00. Status on the receipt for the proceeds being placed in a blocked account is set for January 25, 2010.

Judge Pro Tem: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy McConico.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1076 Tiajuana Street, San Bernardino, Ca)
Off Calendar Oct-19-2009 Continued To Nov-02-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Diana Carloni for Donna Davis.

Ms. Carloni stated that she had a waiver of notice for Clifford Marshall. The matter was continued to November 2, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to allow time for posting.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Off Calendar Jan-14-2009 Granted Per Written Order

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Accounting For The Estate Of Marion Smith
Off Calendar Dec-17-2008 Continued To Jan-14-09 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for Clifford Marshall (Trustee for the Marion Smith). Diana Carloni appeared by telephone for Donna Davis.

Ms. Ng reported that the accounting had not been filed as the matter was resolved and that they were working on the settlement agreement. In addition, she stated that they would be submitting an ex parte petition for approval of the settlement. The matter was continued to january 14, 2009.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: J. Alameda./R. Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Aquino.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Accounting For The Estate Of Marion Smith
Off Calendar Dec-01-2008 Continued To Dec-10-08 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for Clifford Marshall (Trustee of the Estate of Marion Smith). Georgina (Gina) Johnson (Beneficiary) in propria persona. Terry Brooks (Interested Party) in propria persona. Diana Carloni for and with Donna Davis (Beneficiary/Adminstrator of the Estate of Milton Smith Jr.). Gloria Wallace (Beneficiary) in propria persona. Jacque Graham (Beneficiary) in propria persona.

Ms. Ng stated that Ms. Carloni was on her way, that mediation was not successful but the parties were close to settling. The Court stated that the accounting needed to be filed. Ms. Ng stated that the parties were directed by the Commissioner to meet and confer. The matter was then passed until 10:30 a.m. for the arrival of Ms. Carloni.

Upon the arrival of Ms. Carloni settlement discussions were held outside the presence of the Court. At the second call of the matter Ms. Carloni stated the parties had reached a settlement agreement and stated the terms: the parties settled for $70,000.00 which is to be paid in two payments: $30,000.00 to be paid by December 15, 2008 and $40,000.00 to be paid by April 30, 2009, the Estate of Marion Smith waives and assigns any interest in the Estate of Milton Smith to the remaining beneficiaries, if payment is not made by April 30, 2009 interest shall accrue on the balance at five percent per year from December 15, 2008; all due and payable on December 15, 2009, mutual releases are to be signed, and court approval is set by way of an ex-parte hearing set for December 10, 2008 at 10:30 a.m. The Court dispensed with any required notice and stated counsel for the estate could appear by court call.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: J. Alameda./R. Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Accounting For The Estate Of Marion Smith
Off Calendar Sep-08-2008 Continued To Dec-01-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for Clifford Marshall (Trustee of the Estate of Marion Smith). Diana Carloni (by Court Call) for Donna Davis (Administrator). Gloria Wallace (Daughter) in propria persona.

Ms. Carloni presented the Court with an update as to the status of the sale of the real property. She stated that the property was currently listed, and that the real estate agents informed her that it was overpriced. Counsel stated that they were worlds apart as to the accounting, and both agreed to mediation. Based upon counsel's representations the Court referred the matter to Pro Bono Mediation, (signing the order for the same in open court). Mediation is to be completed by November 26, 2008. Ms. Carloni will give notice of the mediation to all parties and beneficiaries. The Court continued the matter to December 1, 2008 at 10:30 a.m. for status on the mediation regarding the accounting of the former administrator. The Court granted Ms. Carloni's request to appear by court call at the next hearing.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy M. Hill.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Accounting For The Estate Of Marion Smith
Off Calendar Aug-04-2008 Continued To Sep-08-08 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Accounting For The Estate Of Marion Smith
Off Calendar Jul-07-2008 Continued To Aug-04-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Jun-30-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for Marion Smith. Diana Carloni appeared by telephone for Donna Davis. Jacquelyn Graham and Gloria Wallace (Daughters) were also present.

Ms. Carloni stated that she filed the bank statements. She also stated that the San Bernadino property fell into a state of disrepair, that they saw no option but to sell as the costs were so significant and that there were no sufficient funds to cover any encumberances. Ms. Ng stated that her client had encouraged the sale of the property from day one. Ms. Graham stated that she would like to see the Marion estate pay back the rents that had been collected for 15 years and that the siblings originally, never wanted to sell the property. Ms. Ng stated that she had no materials to prepare an accounting, that the property was a section 8 housing and that there was a 12 year period where no rent was paid. In addition, she stated that the property had been quit deeded to the Milton estate, that they had a 1/3 interest and that there position was to sell. The Court stated that the estate needed to close, that the property needed to be sold and that an accounting needed to be worked on by the parties. Ms. Carloni will submit a proposed order regarding the property being sold subject to court confirmation and will appear by court call at the next hearing. Ms. Ng is to put together a complete financial picture as possible. The matter was set for July 7, 2008 for status on the accounting for the estate of Marion Smith.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Lazatin.
